title: STOP_SERVERS
text: STOP_SERVERS Stop one or more Uniface Servers. operation STOP_SERVERS params string Srvid : IN ; (I4) string Using : IN ; (C1) string Host : IN ; (C80) string User : IN ; (C40) string UST : IN ; (C20) string Mode : IN ; (C1) endparams Parameters Srvid —ID of the single server to be stopped, if specified. Using —type of server, if specified. One of: S —server with the specified host name, UST, and/or user name C —server used by clients with the specified host name, UST, and/or user name Host —host name of the client or server User —user name of the client or server UST —Uniface Server Type (UST) of the client or server Mode —stop-mode; one of: F for forced or N for normal. In forced mode, servers are stopped immediately and clients are disconnected. In normal mode, existing clients are allowed to deliver requests to servers until the server reports it has no state, that is, it has no locks and no context (active component instances). At that moment, the server is stopped. The selection criteria Host , User and/or UST , if present, are combined (using logical AND) and applied to running servers or clients, to select the servers to be stopped. This operation returns once the request is posted to the Uniface Router. This operation might fail if the servers were already flagged as stopping at an equal or higher mode. Return Values If successful, $status is set to 0. Otherwise, see Return Codes . Use Allowed in all Uniface component types. Description The privileged-user operation STOP_SERVERS stops a single server with the specified Srvid , or stops multiple servers identified by the specified Host , User , and/or UST .
